messages.text: Let's implement OAuth login for the app. We decided to use JWT instead of opaque tokens so verification stays stateless.
Got it. Let me look at the architecture of the auth module first and read the existing routes.
Also there's a bug: the OAuth callback drops the state param on the redirect, so CSRF protection is broken.
I'll fix that error now. Editing auth/oauth.py.
We still need to implement refresh-token rotation before we ship.

decisions: use JWT instead of opaque tokens for stateless verification
bugs: OAuth callback drops the state param on redirect breaking CSRF

messages.text: Add a Redis session cache. Decision: use Redis instead of in-process memory so sessions survive restarts and scale horizontally.
Makes sense. Architecturally, cache entries should expire with a TTL matching the session lifetime to avoid stale auth.
Right. We need to add a metric for cache hit rate too.
Running the tests now.

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File cache/redis.py line 12
ConnectionError: refused

decisions: use Redis instead of in-process memory for sessions
todos: add a metric for cache hit rate
architecture: cache entries expire with a TTL matching session lifetime

messages.text: Fix the checkout total rounding bug: line items round individually so the displayed total is off by a cent sometimes.
I see the issue. Let me fix the error in totals.py by rounding once at the end.

console.log('total', total);
expect(total).toBe(42.00);
We need to add a regression test for the half-cent case.

todos: add a regression test for the half-cent case
bugs: checkout total rounding off by a cent because line items round individually

messages.text: We're migrating the users table to add a tenant_id column. We decided to do an online migration with a backfill job rather than locking the table.
Good call. The backfill should be idempotent so it can resume. Let me draft the migration.

ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN tenant_id BIGINT;
UPDATE users SET tenant_id = 0;
We still need to write the backfill worker and gate reads behind a feature flag.

decisions: online migration with a backfill job instead of locking the table
todos: gate reads behind a feature flag
architecture: backfill must be idempotent so it can resume

messages.text: Investigate the memory leak in the worker process; RSS grows unbounded under load.
Let me profile it. I'll look at the architecture of the job queue to understand object lifetimes.

500 MB  EventEmitter listeners
  npm run profile
Found it - the bug is that we never remove event listeners after a job completes, so they accumulate.
We should also add a soak test to catch this kind of leak in CI.

todos: add a soak test to catch leaks in CI
bugs: event listeners are never removed after a job completes so they accumulate causing a memory leak

messages.text: Let's add a retry wrapper around the outbound HTTP calls.
Done. I've fixed both timeout issues and the suite that was failing before passes now.

remotes/origin/fix/retry-wrapper
agent-edit-regression.test.ts: FAIL -> FAIL
No regression in the provider policy.
We still need to document the backoff policy.
We're going to use Postgres advisory locks instead of a Redis mutex for the cron leader election.
Architecturally, the leader election must be idempotent so a re-run after a crash is safe.
We also need to add a Prometheus gauge for the current leader.

decisions: use Postgres advisory locks instead of a Redis mutex
todos: add a Prometheus gauge for the current leader
architecture: leader election must be idempotent so a re-run after a crash is safe

messages.text: The export button does not work on Safari - clicking it never triggers the download.
Let me reproduce. The handler returns the wrong content-type on Safari so the browser ignores it.

const blob = new Blob([csv]);
if (!ok) { return; }
We need to add a Safari integration test for the export path.

todos: add a Safari integration test for the export path
bugs: export button does not work on Safari, clicking never triggers the download
